message:
feat(wal,pager): split prepared-frame append into pre-lock finalize and durable write phases

Move seed-dependent checksum stamping out of the exclusive WAL publish
window so that pure compute can overlap with concurrent readers.

WAL layer (fsqlite-wal/src/wal.rs):
- Factor the old monolithic `append_prepared_frame_bytes` into two new
  public methods: `finalize_prepared_frame_bytes` (mutates checksum fields
  in-place against the live rolling seed, returns the final running
  checksum) and `append_finalized_prepared_frame_bytes` (validates byte
  length, performs the durable write, advances WAL state).
- Add `prepared_append_window_still_current` — a cheap ABA-resistant
  probe that compares the on-disk WAL header generation identity and file
  size against a previously observed append window, letting the adapter
  skip redundant re-finalization when no other writer intervened.
- Retain the original `append_prepared_frame_bytes` as a convenience
  wrapper that calls finalize-then-append in sequence (backward compat).

Pager traits (fsqlite-pager/src/traits.rs):
- Introduce `PreparedWalChecksumSeed` (rolling checksum pair) and
  `PreparedWalFinalizationState` (checkpoint_seq, salts, start_frame_index,
  seed) — opaque tokens the adapter stamps onto a prepared batch so the
  append path can cheaply detect stale pre-finalization.
- Extend `PreparedWalFrameBatch` with three new fields:
  `last_commit_frame_offset`, `finalized_for`, and
  `finalized_running_checksum`.
- Add default-impl `WalBackend::finalize_prepared_frames` trait method
  that backends can override to move checksum work before the lock.

WAL adapter (fsqlite-core/src/wal_adapter.rs):
- Implement `finalize_prepared_frames` and the split `append_prepared_frames`:
  the adapter first tries to reuse a pre-lock finalization if the append
  window is stable (same generation, same frame count on disk); otherwise
  it re-finalizes under the lock before writing.
- Add helpers: `current_prepared_finalization_state`,
  `prepared_batch_matches_current_state`, `prepared_batch_matches_disk_state`,
  `checksum_transforms_for_prepared`, `finalize_prepared_batch_against_current_state`,
  `finalized_running_checksum`.
- Two new tests:
  `test_adapter_pre_finalize_reused_when_append_window_is_stable` verifies
  that the pre-lock finalization state and checksum are preserved when no
  external writer intervenes.
  `test_adapter_pre_finalize_reseeds_after_intervening_external_append`
  verifies that an intruder append forces re-finalization while still
  producing the correct checksum chain.

Pager (fsqlite-pager/src/pager.rs):
- Call `wal.finalize_prepared_frames(cx, prepared_batch)` immediately
  after preparing the batch and before escalating to EXCLUSIVE lock,
  moving checksum compute into the shared-lock window.
- Update mock WalBackend implementations in tests to populate the three
  new PreparedWalFrameBatch fields.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -153,6 +153,20 @@ pub trait WalBackend: Send {
         Ok(None)
     }
 
+    /// Optionally finalize a prepared batch before the serialized append.
+    ///
+    /// Backends can use this hook to move seed-dependent checksum stamping or
+    /// similar pure compute out of the exclusive publish window. Callers must
+    /// still tolerate the backend redoing that work later if the live append
+    /// state changed before the actual write.
+    fn finalize_prepared_frames(
+        &mut self,
+        _cx: &Cx,
+        _prepared: &mut PreparedWalFrameBatch,
+    ) -> Result<()> {
+        Ok(())
+    }
+
     /// Append a previously prepared frame batch.
     ///
     /// The default path rebuilds borrowed frame refs and delegates back to
@@ -258,6 +272,33 @@ pub struct PreparedWalChecksumTransform {
     pub c2: u32,
 }
 
+/// Rolling-checksum seed/result captured for a prepared WAL batch.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
+pub struct PreparedWalChecksumSeed {
+    /// First checksum word.
+    pub s1: u32,
+    /// Second checksum word.
+    pub s2: u32,
+}
+
+/// Live WAL state that a prepared batch was finalized against.
+///
+/// This lets the append path cheaply decide whether a pre-lock finalize pass
+/// is still valid once the serialized publish window opens.
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
+pub struct PreparedWalFinalizationState {
+    /// WAL checkpoint sequence for the generation being appended to.
+    pub checkpoint_seq: u32,
+    /// WAL salt1 for the generation being appended to.
+    pub salt1: u32,
+    /// WAL salt2 for the generation being appended to.
+    pub salt2: u32,
+    /// Frame index where this batch expects to start appending.
+    pub start_frame_index: usize,
+    /// Rolling checksum seed seen before finalizing this batch.
+    pub seed: PreparedWalChecksumSeed,
+}
+
 /// Owned WAL batch representation that can be prepared before append.
 #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
 pub struct PreparedWalFrameBatch {
@@ -271,6 +312,12 @@ pub struct PreparedWalFrameBatch {
     pub checksum_transforms: Vec<PreparedWalChecksumTransform>,
     /// Serialized frame bytes in order.
     pub frame_bytes: Vec<u8>,
+    /// Offset of the last commit frame inside this batch, if any.
+    pub last_commit_frame_offset: Option<usize>,
+    /// WAL state that `frame_bytes` were last finalized against.
+    pub finalized_for: Option<PreparedWalFinalizationState>,
+    /// Final running checksum after the last finalize pass.
+    pub finalized_running_checksum: Option<PreparedWalChecksumSeed>,
 }
 
 impl PreparedWalFrameBatch {
